dataset: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive Drug Interaction Database

pi_items.source: FDA approval records. Ribociclib (Kisqali) is a CDK4/6 inhibitor approved by FDA for HR-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer. Most recent approval: September 2024 for adjuvant treatment of early breast cancer.
Medical reference databases and pharmaceutical literature. Clarithromycin is a macrolide antibiotic used clinically.
Medical reference databases and pharmaceutical literature. Rifampicin (rifampin) is an anti-tuberculosis drug.

pi_items.verified_value: Clarithromycin causes increased ribociclib toxicity via CYP3A4 inhibition. Clarithromycin is a potent, mechanism-based CYP3A4 inhibitor that increases ribociclib plasma concentration and exposure. This dual mechanism includes: (1) increased ribociclib AUC/Cmax via reduced hepatic/intestinal metabolism, and (2) additive QTc prolongation risk (both drugs prolong QT interval). Clinical consequence: increased toxicity requiring dose reduction of ribociclib to 400 mg/day if coadministration cannot be avoided.
pi_items.source: Multiple peer-reviewed sources: (1) Medscape/RxList drug interaction databases confirm clarithromycin increases ribociclib effect via CYP3A inhibition. (2) FDA drug interaction guidance documents. (3) PubMed literature on mechanism-based CYP3A4 inhibition by clarithromycin causing increased plasma concentrations of substrates. (4) Cardiac safety literature on QTc prolongation with clarithromycin and ribociclib combination.

pi_items.verified_value: Rifampicin causes decreased ribociclib efficacy via CYP3A4 induction. Rifampicin is a potent CYP3A4 inducer that substantially decreases ribociclib plasma concentration and bioavailability. Specific quantitative data: rifampicin (600 mg daily for 14 days) decreases ribociclib Cmax by 81% and AUC by 89% following a single 600 mg ribociclib dose. Clinical consequence: significantly reduced therapeutic efficacy of ribociclib. Clinical recommendation is to avoid concomitant use or consider alternate medications with minimal CYP3A4 induction potential.
pi_items.source: PubMed reference 32557601 (Ribociclib Drug-Drug Interactions: Clinical Evaluations and Physiologically-Based Pharmacokinetic Modeling to Guide Drug Labeling). FDA drug labeling and interaction guidance. Multiple pharmacokinetic interaction studies documenting the 89% AUC reduction with rifampicin coadministration.
Task definition in task.json and confirmed by setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts which reference this exact file path.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses real medical data from the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive, a drug interaction database developed by the University of Liverpool and Radboud UMC. All metadata claims are scientifically accurate: (1) Ribociclib is a real FDA-approved CDK4/6 inhibitor cancer drug used for breast cancer. (2) Clarithromycin is a confirmed CYP3A4 inhibitor that increases ribociclib exposure and toxicity risk, with clinical recommendations to reduce ribociclib dose or avoid concomitant use. (3) Rifampicin is a confirmed potent CYP3A4 inducer that decreases ribociclib bioavailability by 89% (AUC reduction), significantly reducing therapeutic efficacy. The task requires agents to correctly identify these opposing mechanisms: increased toxicity/exposure for one drug, and decreased efficacy/exposure for the other.
